Los Pelones.description: Los Pelones is an enforcer gang originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel, founded in 2004 in Guerrero, Mexico. It distributed narcotics in tourist spots and later became independent after the Beltrán-Leyva split. The group allied with the Gulf Cartel in 2012 and later became independent again due to fragmentation. It is involved in drug trafficking, murder, and extortion.

descriptions: Los Pelones were former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones in Morelos. These are former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva brothers, now part of the Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Los Pelones is a splinter group of Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones en el Estado de México, Chiapas, Quintana Roo y Morelos.

descriptions: Los Pelones emerged from fragmentation of Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones emerged as an independent cartel during this fragmentation as well.

descriptions: Los Pelones lists Gente Nueva as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gente Nueva

descriptions: Los Pelones announced they were joining the Gulf Cartel in 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: On December 27, 2012, the group announced that they had stopped operating independently and that now they are part of the "honorable" Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Los Pelones are now part of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones in Morelos. These are former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva brothers, now part of the Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Los Pelones was originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pelones ("The Bald Ones") is an enforcer gang originally part of the Mexican drug trafficking organization known as the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: The PKK co-founded the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other groups.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP , TKEP/L , TİKB , DKP , DK and MLKP ) with the aim of overthrowing the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

descriptions: The PKK was part of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: The PKK has in March 2016 also vowed to overthrow the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, through the 'Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement'.

La Raza Nation.description: La Raza Nation is a street gang founded in Chicago in 1973, primarily composed of Hispanic members of Mexican or Central American origin. Its colors are green, white, and red, and its symbols include the Mexican flag, cross, and six-point star. The gang is affiliated with the Folk Nation and has been involved in conflicts with various other gangs. It has expanded from Chicago to other states including Illinois, Wisconsin, Ohio, Florida, and Georgia.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , La Raza Nation , Gangster Two Six , Satan's Disciples , Latin Kings

Noua Dreaptă.description: Ultranationalist, far-right organization in Romania and Moldova, founded in 2000. Claims to be successor to the Iron Guard, promoting militant ultranationalism and Orthodox Christianity. Opposes sexual minorities, Roma, abortion, communism, globalization, EU, NATO, and non-Orthodox religions. Supports merger of Romania and Moldova.

descriptions: Noua Dreaptă claims to be the successor of the Iron Guard.
edges.evidence_quote: The party claims to be the successor to the far-right Iron Guard

descriptions: Sexy Boys is a smaller unit within The Americans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Americans gang is made up of smaller units such as the Ugly Americans, Young Americans, Spoilt Brats, Dollar Boys, Dollar Kids, Young Dixie Boys and Sexy Boys.

Petit Bar Gang.description: The Petit Bar Gang is a Corsican organized crime group named after a bar in Ajaccio. It engages in extortion, money laundering, and other criminal activities, with influence in Corsica, mainland France, and abroad. Considered the most powerful criminal group in Corsica as of 2022, it has established political and economic networks. The group was founded in the 2000s and remains active.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
descriptions: The infobox lists Petit Bar Gang as a rival of Brise de Mer.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Petit Bar Gang , Venzolasca Gang , Armata Corsa

Zetas Vieja Escuela.description: Mexican criminal organization that splintered from Los Zetas in 2014, founded by José Guizar Valencia (Z-43) and Antonio Salas Perea. Based in northern Veracruz, it operates in multiple Mexican states and engages in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, murder, and oil theft. The group claims to continue the original brutal business of Los Zetas.

descriptions: CJNG ejecutó a un miembro de Zetas Vieja Escuela, indicando conflicto.
edges.evidence_quote: En marzo del 2019, miembros del Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación ejecutaron a un miembro de los Zetas Vieja Escuela, posiblemente en el estado de Veracruz.

Kyokuto-kai.description: The Kyokuto-kai is a yakuza organization based in Tokyo, Japan, founded by Aiji Sekiguchi. It was registered as a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law in July 1993. In the early 2000s, it was involved in a feud with the rival Matsuba-kai, leading to shootings. As of the article, it has about 300 members and is led by Jin Takahashi.

descriptions: Kyokuto-kai and Matsuba-kai were involved in a feud that led to shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2000s, the Kyokuto-kai was involved in a feud with the rival Matsuba-kai , which led to a number of shootings.

descriptions: Matsuba-kai and Kyokuto-kai were involved in a violent feud in the early 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 2000s the Matsuba-kai was involved in a violent feud with the rival Kyokuto-kai , which led to a number of shootings.

edges.evidence_quote: On 24 October 2022, during the Russo-Ukrainian War, the club formed a volunteer unit which joined the Pyatnashka International Brigade.
